meta_title: For Founders: idea validation to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to idea validation and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Idea Validation to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Stop worrying about your portfolio. Learn how to validate your AI startup idea rapidly using Antigravity and Claude Code in this expert-led beginner guide.
content.answer_block: You don't need a portfolio; you need a paying customer. Skip the vanity projects and build a high-velocity MVP. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative development, you can move from a raw idea to a market-tested prototype in hours, not weeks.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the graveyard of ambition. You feel paralyzed, staring at a portfolio that is empty, convinced that without a track record, you cannot command the market. This is a fallacy that keeps thousands of founders stuck in the 'tutorial hell' loop, building apps that nobody wants and nobody buys.

True engineering authority isn't about the number of repos on your GitHub; it's about the speed at which you can translate a market pain point into a functional, revenue-generating solution. We are going to strip away the noise, leverage AI-native tools, and force the market to tell you exactly what to build.

steps.title: Define the Value Prop Hypothesis
steps.description: Before writing code, define the specific pain point your AI solves. Focus on a narrow niche where you can charge immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a SaaS architect. Generate a one-page lean canvas for an AI tool that solves [INSERT NICHE PROBLEM]. Focus on the primary value proposition and the first 3 features needed for a paid MVP.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a project README.md outlining the core value proposition and the specific problem we are solving for our first 10 beta users.'

steps.title: Rapid Scaffolding with Antigravity
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the boilerplate. Focus on authentication, database schema, and the AI API integration layer.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a Next.js application with Tailwind CSS, Supabase for auth/db, and an OpenAI API route handler. Ensure the structure is modular for rapid feature iteration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize the project structure based on the Antigravity scaffold and verify all environment variables are set.'

steps.title: Build the 'Concierge' MVP
steps.description: Don't build the whole product. Build only the interface that delivers the core AI output. Manual intervention is fine for the first 5 users.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a clean, minimal UI for a single-input prompt interface that feeds into an AI processing function and displays the result in a card component.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Build the frontend input component and connect it to the backend AI proxy route.'

steps.title: Implement Feedback Loops
steps.description: Add a simple 'Thumbs Up/Down' or 'Copy to Clipboard' feature to track if the AI output is actually useful to the user.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add a feedback logging system to the AI route that saves user prompts and output quality ratings to the Supabase database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Add a feedback component to the UI and create a database migration for storing user interaction metrics.'

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Get it live. A local app is invisible. A URL on the internet is a business.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide the Vercel deployment configuration and a checklist for pre-launch security checks including rate limiting on the AI API route.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Perform a final security audit on the codebase and prepare the deployment script for Vercel.'

pro_tips: Never build authentication from scratch; use Clerk or Supabase Auth to save 20 hours of development time.
The first 10 users are more valuable than the first 100 features; talk to them daily.
If you can't describe your product in one sentence, you haven't validated the idea yet.
Use 'feature flagging' to hide unfinished code so you can deploy to production continuously.
Focus on the 'Time-to-Value'—how many seconds from landing on the site to getting the AI result?
content.next_steps: Once deployed, share your URL in three relevant communities (Reddit, X, or niche Slack groups) and iterate based on the feedback logs you implemented in Step 4.
content.conversion_block: Stop building for your resume and start building for revenue. Your portfolio grows when your users succeed. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
